results.summary: JPM remains the primary Big Bank anchor on scale, profitability, and valuation quality. BAC is the clean secondary candidate: cheaper than JPM, with a solid Q1 beat and constructive Reuters-linked expectation readthrough. Overall read is constructive, with the group still anchored by quality franchises rather than deep-value distress.

forward_expectations.readthrough: Forward estimates still imply a durable low-teens earnings profile and continued premium-but-reasonable valuation support if JPM keeps executing.

notable_news: May 27, 2026 8-K redemption of Series KK preferred depositary shares
Recent news flow remains constructive on scale, capital access, and franchise strength
Q1 2026 filing remains the core fundamental anchor for the sweep

latest_8_k.note: May 27, 2026 redemption of Series KK preferred depositary shares (per the supplied brief).

latest_quarter.readthrough: Reuters-linked expectations were for a solid quarter; BAC delivered a clear EPS beat and held revenue in constructive shape.

latest_10_q.note: Latest Q1 2026 filing (exact URL not surfaced in the supplied brief).

Any later capital-return or preferred-related update referenced in the briefing.

notes: BAC remains the cleaner value/comp-rounding play after JPM.
The earnings beat supports the Reuters-linked constructive readthrough.
P/B at 1.24 keeps BAC on the value side of the group, even if JPM remains the higher-quality anchor.

peer_context.note: Lower P/B and still-improving execution keep Citigroup in the value/catch-up conversation.
Cheap, but still more of a proof story than a premium-quality story.
Higher multiple, but quality/franchise mix remains strong.
Premium valuation remains justified by wealth-management and capital-markets strength.

assessment.summary: Bottom line: JPM stays the quality leader and morning anchor; BAC is the best secondary value/earnings-beat candidate. The broader Big Bank tape remains constructive rather than broken.
bull_points: JPM combines scale, profitability, and valuation quality with a still-reasonable P/E and P/B.
BAC delivered a Q1 EPS beat and remains cheaper than JPM on both P/E and P/B.
Forward expectations still support durable earnings power for JPM if execution holds.
bear_points: BAC is still the more value-sensitive name and needs continued execution to sustain multiple expansion.
Bank headlines can skew noisy, so filing-level confirmation remains important.
Rate and credit conditions remain the key macro cross-currents for the group.
